title: One Battle vs. F1: How the ACE Eddie Awards can provide clarity for the Best Film Editing Oscar race
text: The Best Film Editing Oscar category remains highly competitive, with different films winning various precursor awards. The American Cinema Editors (ACE) Awards may help settle the debate. The ACE Eddie Awards have proven relatively reliable in predicting Academy Award winners. One of its winners has matched the Academy Award for Best Film Editing 17 times across 26 years, representing a 65 percent accuracy rate. Oscar Predictions (Gold Derby): One Battle After Another leads with 78 percent support, F1 follows at 16 percent. ACE Eddie Predictions (Gold Derby): F1 leads Best Film Drama Editing at 87 percent, One Battle After Another dominates Best Film Comedy/Musical Editing at 95+ percent. If both films win their respective ACE categories as predicted, it wouldn't clarify the Oscar race. Since One Battle After Another is fundamentally an action film despite its categorization, it could have stronger momentum at the Oscars. The ACE Eddie Awards were held Friday at UCLA's Royce Hall, with the Oscars following on March 15.
